data.title: Update project docs to reflect review decision gate changes
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update existing project documentation to reflect the new fragile severity, ESCALATE_REVIEW signal, one-way ratchet, classifier floor rule, and approach_viability_concern signal.
**Why**: CLAUDE.md and REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md are authoritative references for agent behavior — stale documentation causes agents to use outdated patterns.
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md architecture section (agent routing table, review pipeline description, new signal documentation), KNOWN-ISSUES.md (if new operational patterns emerge during implementation)
- OUT: REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md updates are handled by Stories 3 and 4 as part of their implementation.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md documents the ESCALATE_REVIEW signal, one-way ratchet behavior, fragile severity semantics, approach_viability_concern signal, and classifier external-API floor rule
  ← Satisfies: all epic success criteria (documentation traceability)
- When this story is complete, the CLAUDE.md agent routing table includes any new agents or signal-dispatched behaviors introduced by this epic
  ← Satisfies: all epic success criteria (documentation traceability)
- When this story is complete, all documentation updates follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md formatting and conventions
  ← Satisfies: documentation quality standard

## Considerations

- [Adversarial] CLAUDE.md documentation must be specific about new signals — listing signal names, which agents emit them, which orchestrators consume them, and the routing behavior they trigger

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
